title: MySQL/MariaDB Binlog Streaming Flow

EventBuffer.generic: T extends BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource, P extends BinlogPartition, O extends BinlogOffsetContext

interaction_flow: BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource → eventHandlers: register handlers for STOP, HEARTBEAT, INCIDENT, ROTATE, TABLE_MAP, QUERY, WRITE_ROWS, UPDATE_ROWS, DELETE_ROWS, XID, TRANSACTION_PAYLOAD
BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource → EventBuffer: new EventBuffer(bufferSize, this, context) [if bufferSize > 0]
BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource → BinaryLogClient: registerEventListener(event -> onEvent(offsetContext, event)) [metrics tracking]
--- STREAMING LOOP (main thread parks, BinaryLogClient fires events) ---
MySQLServer → BinaryLogClient: raw binlog bytes (background thread)
BinaryLogClient → onEvent listener: onEvent(offsetContext, event) [metrics: lag calc, setMilliSecondsBehindSource]
BinaryLogClient → EventBuffer: add(partition, offsetContext, event) [if buffering enabled]
EventBuffer → EventBuffer: check isReplayingEventsBeyondBufferCapacity() -> if true, direct handleEvent
EventBuffer: QUERY/BEGIN → beginTransaction(partition, offsetContext, event) → addToBuffer(event)
EventBuffer: DML event (not in tx) → consumeEvent() → streamingChangeEventSource.handleEvent()
EventBuffer: DML event (in tx) → consumeEvent() → addToBuffer(event)
EventBuffer: QUERY/COMMIT or XID → completeTransaction(partition, offsetContext, wellFormed=true, event)
EventBuffer.completeTransaction → iterate buffer → streamingChangeEventSource.handleEvent() for each buffered event
--- DML EVENT HANDLING (INSERT example, same pattern for UPDATE/DELETE) ---
BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource.handleInsert → handleChange(partition, offsetContext, event, CREATE, WriteRowsEventData, tableIdProvider, rowsProvider, changeEmitter, validator)
handleChange → EventDispatcher: dispatchDataChangeEvent(partition, tableId, new BinlogChangeRecordEmitter(partition, offsetContext, clock, CREATE, null, row, connectorConfig))
BinlogStreamingChangeEventSourceMetrics → BinlogEventMetadataProvider: getEventTimestamp(source, offset, key, value)
BinlogEventMetadataProvider → value.getStruct(SOURCE): reads TIMESTAMP_KEY -> returns Instant
BinlogStreamingChangeEventSourceMetrics → BinlogEventMetadataProvider: getEventSourcePosition(source, offset, key, value)
BinlogEventMetadataProvider → value.getStruct(SOURCE): reads binlogFilename, binlogPosition, rowInEvent -> returns Map
BinlogStreamingChangeEventSourceMetrics → BinlogEventMetadataProvider: getTransactionId(source, offset, key, value)
BinaryLogClient → ReaderThreadLifecycleListener: onEventDeserializationFailure(client, ex) -> ErrorHandler.setProducerThrowable / log per mode
BinaryLogClient → ReaderThreadLifecycleListener: onDisconnect(client) -> log last offset
ChangeEventSourceContext.isRunning() returns false -> main loop exits
key_relationships: BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource composes EventBuffer (optional, for large-tx buffering)
BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource contains inner class ReaderThreadLifecycleListener implements BinaryLogClient.LifecycleListener
BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource contains static inner class BinlogPosition
EventBuffer delegates event handling back to BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource.handleEvent()
handleInsert/Update/Delete all delegate to private handleChange() which calls EventDispatcher.dispatchDataChangeEvent()
handleChange creates BinlogChangeRecordEmitter per row per DML event
important_notes: EventBuffer is optional: enabled only when bufferSizeForStreamingChangeEventSource > 0 in config
EventBuffer purpose: filter out rolled-back transactions (e.g., temp table drops, DBZ-390)
Large transaction overflow: EventBuffer records largeTxNotBufferedPosition, flushes buffer, calls rewindBinaryLogClient() to re-read from that position
GTID mode: if enabled, uses setGtidSet() instead of setBinlogFilename/Position; filtered GtidSet computed from server + stored offset
Event routing uses EnumMap<EventType, BlockingConsumer<Event>> registered in execute(); unregistered types go to ignoreEvent()
Offset commit is implicit: offsetContext.completeEvent() called after every event in handleEvent()
offsetContext.changeEventCompleted() called after each DML row batch in handleChange()
Streaming paused check: waitWhenStreamingPaused(context) called at start of every handleEvent iteration
BinlogEventMetadataProvider reads source position fields: BINLOG_FILENAME_OFFSET_KEY, BINLOG_POSITION_OFFSET_KEY, BINLOG_ROW_IN_EVENT_OFFSET_KEY
ReaderThreadLifecycleListener.onConnect() fires on BinaryLogClient background thread, not main thread
Error in handleEvent: handlers cleared, errorHandler.setProducerThrowable() called; client NOT disconnected (left to Kafka Connect)
